chunks.content: Solvency Capital Requirement (SCR). The SCR shall correspond to the 99.5% VaR of the basic own funds of an insurance or reinsurance undertaking over a one-year period. It may be calculated using the Standard Formula or an approved internal model.

Minimum Capital Requirement (MCR). The MCR shall never fall below 25% of the SCR (or 45% for certain undertakings). Absolute floor: €3.7M for life, €2.5M for non-life. Solvency ratio = (Eligible own funds / SCR) * 100%; must be >= 100%.
